column19: Scheme Twists in the Villain Deck
column23: Comments

column2: Morgan le Fay's Demon Bear Saga
column23: Patriot and two Caps combo made wonders! Left hand side managed to grab Patriot's rare early in the game and everytime I have used it suprisingly worked. Two uncommon Wasps on the right side made the first blow on Morgan with the help of some recruit. Last turn was amazing - Left managed to hit Morgan le Fay 4 times and defeated Yeoman America thanks to 25 attack from Cap Core (and his rare of course), 7 attack from Patriot and 7 attack from Secret Avenger Cap (only from his uncommon)!
Deployed the Black Widow with and all tech AntMan….Black Widow is a chivalrous duel cheat code

I thought I could do better the second try. Turns out I was wrong. Couldn't get the chivalrous duel attack done. First round actually went really good with an early pick up of Wasps rare. Gladiator Hulk also proved helpful with the second hand. Here's to hoping it will be enough!

This scheme with only 3 special bystanders is not as fun. I decided to bring in some Spiders to support Scarlet and fill in the 2 cost gap. It worked pretty well. Didn't see any rares the whole game though so that was disappointing. But Annihilus was nice enough use his masterstrikes to capture chimichangas for him self so that was lucky. I also drew his tactics in a fortunate order. The first game didn't go very well though, too many bystander chains into scheme twists. The city can really fill in fast.

Both runs were very similar, i was able to feed villains to Galactus by moving them with Storm. So i am submitting the slightly faster one. It's a shame such bystander-heavy setup had only 3 special ones.
I hope that Annihilus enjoyed that goddamn chimichanga.

column2: Baron Heinrich Zemo's Alien Brood Encounters
column23: Player 1 used Unseen Rescue, Covert Operations, and One Gigantic Hand to attack the mastermind. Player 2 used low attack turns to keep scanning the city. We took advantage of KO'ing scheme twists from our hands/discard piles whenever possible. Another fun, challenging game of Legendary!
I love finding new interactions with cards I've already played a lot of.

My first game, I completely forgot about being able to pay 2 recruit whenever I fight a villain to rescue a bystander! This game though, Rogue was copying Lady Thor’s rare a bunch and Mr. Fantastic had big ol’ hands thanks to Black Widow’s “Mission Accomplished.” Maximus being able to defeat the Brood for free was a game changer too.
I am inevitable

Spider Deck on the left with a copy of Elektra's Uncommon let me blow away 2 lurking Masterminds and half of a third in a single turn!! Technically the other game had a higher PPT, but this one was better in both bystander count and raw turn count.

Since barely any KO existed, I had to supply the deck with a lot of it, but also needed a lot of power for what I was up against. Spider-Man turned out to be actually reasonable once I included two characters with 2's. Moon Knight barely showed up.
Second game wasn’t as good as the first

Wild game setup, the mix of different MM's and Villains created a few different angles of attack.  My picks were based on top-deck manipulation to synergize with Moon Knight and Spiderman.  Comic side stuck mostly with Spiderman to get 2 or 0's and used Blind Justice and Stack The Deck to clear off things higher than 2.  MCU side used Hypnotic Charm to help out both decks and concentrated on Crescent Moon Darts and stacking Instinct.
Angel's discard engine worked well for me, but the Transformed Illuminati really tripped me up and left many of my strategies futile. Definitely not the MM to try a drawing deck on.

column2: Zheng Zhu Floods the Planet with Melted Glaciers
column23: You gave Kylah Angel and Iron Fist in the same game......What else did we expect? Ollie collected Lady Thors, who did Lady Thor things...but it just couldn't compare.

Come on! The Deadlands again!!! Together with Ghost Racers!! So many "Rise of the Living Dead"-Villains meant, I needed extra Bystanders for my Victory Piles to prevent their resurrection. I sighed and finally chose Black Widow - luckily I saved her for THAT kind of matches. Punisher was the other obvious choice: First: MK affiliation and Second: he should have fun with that high VP villains on top of the the Villain Deck. And it all paid off! Although I saw many twists much too early I had two very strong hands. Only Thor was out of place this time. Too bad I had these 2 early escapes. But I'm sure I won't be on the last place with that score :-)

Player 1 was able to nab Silver Surfer’s rare and focus draw common early on and powered through, drawing the whole deck every other turn, and hitting the mastermind 3 times in the last turn, while Player 2 just sat back shuffling his deck with Gambit.
Funny how different games play out. First match was a ton of Diving Catch. Second game was all God of Thunder just wrecking stuff with not a single Diving Catch in the HQ.

I was so worried I was gonna lose after Zombie Thanos came up 3 turn, even ended up ascending. But I liked the synergy of the group and ended up winning. However I think this may be the end of my tournament.

column2: Apocalypse Turns the Soul of Adam Warlock
column23: Had to pull out the big guns to combat my fear of Immortal and Undefeated. In the game I didn't submit, I randomly pulled it 5 times in a row before Adam Warlock had his soul corrupted.
Storm and Siryn worked really well with all the blue and red, and with Speedy's rare, it helped gather up the Hyperspeed attack I needed.
Poor luck with both Villain and Hero decks left us stranded in the Quantum Realm. But this tournament was really fun overall! Thanks Bagel!

Apocalypse + Turn the Soul of Adam Warlock = pain. I was 3 attack away from hitting him for his final hit on the last turn. I even purified the last five Adam Warlocks but it didn't end up mattering. Thanks for the memories tournament friends! Best of luck to my fellow competitors.
